issue_title: Adding the APC UPS Daemon (apcupsd) integration fails during configuration
issue_body:
### The problem

When adding the APC UPS Daemon integration, the configuration dialog stalls for a minute after clicking submit, finally producing an error message 'Unknown error occurred'.

![image](https://github.com/home-assistant/core/assets/885571/301fa765-866d-43d5-b219-41f5004d7d90)
![image](https://github.com/home-assistant/core/assets/885571/bc1bcfc8-990a-4ead-a93e-fa16d69b30da)

The apcupsd daemon is running on a different machine (192.168.1.211) and I have confirmed the apcupsd daemon is accessible on port 3551 from other machines on the LAN as I have tested the aioapcaccess python module is indeed connecting over the LAN with no issues from other machines.

code:
```
import asyncio
import aioapcaccess

async def main():
    result = await aioapcaccess.request_status(host='192.168.1.211', port=3551)
    print(result)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    asyncio.run(main())
```

result:
```
OrderedDict([('APC', '001,036,0888'), ('DATE', '2024-01-17 12:57:48 +0800'), ('HOSTNAME', 'redacted'), ('VERSION', '3.14.14 (31 May 2016) slackware'), ('UPSNAME', 'CharlieMurphy'), ('CABLE', 'USB Cable'), ('DRIVER', 'USB UPS Driver'), ('UPSMODE', 'Stand Alone'), ('STARTTIME', '2024-01-14 00:00:02 +0800'), ('MODEL', 'Back-UPS RS 550GI'), ('STATUS', 'ONLINE'), ('LINEV', '239.0 Volts'), ('LOADPCT', '31.0 Percent'), ('BCHARGE', '100.0 Percent'), ('TIMELEFT', '16.3 Minutes'), ('MBATTCHG', '0 Percent'), ('MINTIMEL', '0 Minutes'), ('MAXTIME', '0 Seconds'), ('SENSE', 'Medium'), ('LOTRANS', '176.0 Volts'), ('HITRANS', '282.0 Volts'), ('ALARMDEL', 'No alarm'), ('BATTV', '13.6 Volts'), ('LASTXFER', 'Unacceptable line voltage changes'), ('NUMXFERS', '0'), ('TONBATT', '0 Seconds'), ('CUMONBATT', '0 Seconds'), ('XOFFBATT', 'N/A'), ('SELFTEST', 'NO'), ('STATFLAG', '0x05000008'), ('SERIALNO', 'redacted'), ('BATTDATE', '2012-07-14'), ('NOMINV', '230 Volts'), ('NOMBATTV', '12.0 Volts'), ('NOMPOWER', '330 Watts'), ('FIRMWARE', '857.L3 .I USB FW:L3'), ('END APC', '2024-01-17 12:57:56 +0800')])
```


### What version of Home Assistant Core has the issue?

core-2024.1.2

### What was the last working version of Home Assistant Core?

_No response_

### What type of installation are you running?

Home Assistant OS

### Integration causing the issue

apcupsd

### Link to integration documentation on our website

https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/apcupsd

### Diagnostics information

_No response_

### Example YAML snippet

_No response_

### Anything in the logs that might be useful for us?

```txt
2024-01-17 16:19:10.919 DEBUG (MainThread) [homeassistant.components.websocket_api.http.connection] [140166922618816] thejonesyboy from 127.0.0.1 (Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36): Received {'type': 'manifest/get', 'integration': 'apcupsd', 'id': 51}

2024-01-17 16:19:10.920 DEBUG (MainThread) [homeassistant.components.websocket_api.http.connection] [140166922618816] thejonesyboy  from 127.0.0.1 (Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36): Sending {"id":51,"type":"result","success":true,"result":{"domain":"apcupsd","name":"APC UPS Daemon","codeowners":["@yuxincs"],"config_flow":true,"documentation":"https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/apcupsd","iot_class":"local_polling","loggers":["apcaccess"],"quality_scale":"silver","requirements":["aioapcaccess==0.4.2"],"is_built_in":true}}

2024-01-17 16:20:16.840 DEBUG (MainThread) [homeassistant.components.apcupsd.coordinator] Finished fetching apcupsd data in 0.002 seconds (success: True)
```


### Additional information

_No response_
pr_title: Fix stalls in config flow of APCUPSD
pr_body:
## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->

This PR fixes the stalls in config flow of APCUPSD integration, where we were mistakenly calling `hass.async_block_till_done()` after requesting initial coordinator refresh. 

`hass.async_block_till_done()` waits for _everything_ in HASS, not just the the integration itself (and it is usually only used in test code). Therefore, this call will probably never return once HASS startup finishes. We were unable to reproduce this since we were testing on new installations, but users with existing installations have reported such an issue when they were adding an apcupsd integration (see #108221).

Credit goes to @dom42 who was able to reproduce this locally and found the culprit.

## Type of change
<!--
  What type of change does your PR introduce to Home Assistant?
  NOTE: Please, check only 1! box!
  If your PR requires multiple boxes to be checked, you'll most likely need to
  split it into multiple PRs. This makes things easier and faster to code review.
-->

- [ ] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

-->
